chunks.text: The EECS Department offers students the opportunity to receive a truly outstanding education in the broad field of information science and technology. Your generous support can help sustain and expand our public university programs to prepare all of our students to become tomorrow’s engineering leaders.
chunks.question: What does supporting EECS help achieve?
chunks.answer: Sustaining and expanding public university programs.

chunks.text: Support the Gier Memorial Project. A community is defined by the heroes it chooses to celebrate. We invite you to join the EECS department in recognizing a previously overlooked hero, Berkeley EE Prof. Joseph T. Gier, the University of California’s first tenured Black professor.
chunks.question: Who is the Gier Memorial Project honoring?

chunks.text: A gift to the EECS Excellence Fund extends Berkeley’s long-standing leadership and excellence in education and research in Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences.
chunks.question: What does the EECS Excellence Fund support?

chunks.text: Your donation to CS Scholars supports our members by providing funding for academic opportunities such as tutoring and advising, community-building opportunities such as peer mentoring, professional development, and funding for students to travel to attend national research conferences.
chunks.question: What does CS Scholars funding provide?

chunks.text: Help recruit teachers and guest speakers, host students, and develop curricular materials for a one-week class designed to introduce inexperienced students to computer science.
chunks.question: What is the CS Kickstart Program?
chunks.answer: A one-week computer science introduction class.

chunks.text: If you have any questions about making a gift to EECS, please contact gifts@eecs.berkeley.edu.
chunks.question: How to contact about EECS gifts?
